reforms.description: The Swiss government introduced a federal order to provide financial assistance for the adjustment of one-industry regions, particularly in watchmaking, through preferential loans and guarantee schemes to facilitate innovation and diversification projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The financial assistance aims to promote innovation and diversification, which can enhance economic resilience and productivity in affected regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into action as indicated by its implemented status.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a federal order represents a significant structural change in supporting innovation in specific regions.
reforms.source_quote: A federal order adopted on 6th October, 1978 and due to come into force in the spring of 1979 provides for financial assistance to facilitate the adjustment of basically one-industry regions.

reforms.description: The 'impetus' programme was adopted to promote technological development and training in electronics, providing government aid for quality assessment, software training, and improvement of electronic components.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to enhance technological capabilities and workforce skills in electronics, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively providing aid as indicated by its implemented status.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the programme is more focused and does not represent a system-wide change.
reforms.source_quote: In December 1978, the Federal Parliament adopted a series of measures to promote technological development and training (the 'impetus' programme).
